tools.name: bots.batchCreateCustomVocabularyItem
tools.description: Create a batch of custom vocabulary items for a given bot locale's custom vocabulary.

tools.name: bots.batchDeleteCustomVocabularyItem
tools.description: Delete a batch of custom vocabulary items for a given bot locale's custom vocabulary.

tools.name: bots.batchUpdateCustomVocabularyItem
tools.description: Update a batch of custom vocabulary items for a given bot locale's custom vocabulary.

tools.name: bots.buildBotLocale
tools.description: Builds a bot, its intents, and its slot types into a specific locale. A bot can be built into multiple locales. At runtime the locale is used to choose a specific build of the bot.

tools.name: bots.createBot
tools.description: Creates an Amazon Lex conversational bot.

tools.name: bots.createBotAlias
tools.description:
Creates an alias for the specified version of a bot. Use an alias to enable you to change the version of a bot without updating applications that use the bot.

For example, you can create an alias called "PROD" that your applications use to call the Amazon Lex bot.

tools.name: bots.createBotLocale
tools.description: Creates a locale in the bot. The locale contains the intents and slot types that the bot uses in conversations with users in the specified language and locale. You must add a locale to a bot before you can add intents and slot types to the bot.

tools.name: bots.createBotVersion
tools.description:
Creates a new version of the bot based on the DRAFT version. If the DRAFT version of this resource hasn't changed since you created the last version, Amazon Lex doesn't create a new version, it returns the last created version.

When you create the first version of a bot, Amazon Lex sets the version to 1. Subsequent versions increment by 1.

tools.name: bots.createIntent
tools.description:
Creates an intent.

To define the interaction between the user and your bot, you define one or more intents. For example, for a pizza ordering bot you would create an OrderPizza intent.

When you create an intent, you must provide a name. You can optionally provide the following:

  • Sample utterances. For example, "I want to order a pizza" and "Can I order a pizza." You can't provide utterances for built-in intents.

  • Information to be gathered. You specify slots for the information that you bot requests from the user. You can specify standard slot types, such as date and time, or custom slot types for your application.

  • How the intent is fulfilled. You can provide a Lambda function or configure the intent to return the intent information to your client application. If you use a Lambda function, Amazon Lex invokes the function when all of the intent information is available.

  • A confirmation prompt to send to the user to confirm an intent. For example, "Shall I order your pizza?"

  • A conclusion statement to send to the user after the intent is fulfilled. For example, "I ordered your pizza."

  • A follow-up prompt that asks the user for additional activity. For example, "Do you want a drink with your pizza?"

tools.name: bots.createSlot
tools.description: Creates a slot in an intent. A slot is a variable needed to fulfill an intent. For example, an OrderPizza intent might need slots for size, crust, and number of pizzas. For each slot, you define one or more utterances that Amazon Lex uses to elicit a response from the user.

tools.name: bots.createSlotType
tools.description:
Creates a custom slot type

To create a custom slot type, specify a name for the slot type and a set of enumeration values, the values that a slot of this type can assume.

tools.name: bots.deleteBot
tools.description:
Deletes all versions of a bot, including the Draft version. To delete a specific version, use the DeleteBotVersion operation.

When you delete a bot, all of the resources contained in the bot are also deleted. Deleting a bot removes all locales, intents, slot, and slot types defined for the bot.

If a bot has an alias, the DeleteBot operation returns a ResourceInUseException exception. If you want to delete the bot and the alias, set the skipResourceInUseCheck parameter to true.

tools.name: bots.deleteBotAlias
tools.description: Deletes the specified bot alias.

tools.name: bots.deleteBotLocale
tools.description:
Removes a locale from a bot.

When you delete a locale, all intents, slots, and slot types defined for the locale are also deleted.

tools.name: bots.deleteBotVersion
tools.description: Deletes a specific version of a bot. To delete all versions of a bot, use the DeleteBot operation.

tools.name: bots.deleteCustomVocabulary
tools.description: Removes a custom vocabulary from the specified locale in the specified bot.

tools.name: bots.deleteIntent
tools.description:
Removes the specified intent.

Deleting an intent also deletes the slots associated with the intent.

tools.name: bots.deleteSlot
tools.description: Deletes the specified slot from an intent.

tools.name: bots.deleteSlotType
tools.description:
Deletes a slot type from a bot locale.

If a slot is using the slot type, Amazon Lex throws a ResourceInUseException exception. To avoid the exception, set the skipResourceInUseCheck parameter to true.

tools.name: bots.deleteUtterances
tools.description:
Deletes stored utterances.

Amazon Lex stores the utterances that users send to your bot. Utterances are stored for 15 days for use with the ListAggregatedUtterances operation, and then stored indefinitely for use in improving the ability of your bot to respond to user input..

Use the DeleteUtterances operation to manually delete utterances for a specific session. When you use the DeleteUtterances operation, utterances stored for improving your bot's ability to respond to user input are deleted immediately. Utterances stored for use with the ListAggregatedUtterances operation are deleted after 15 days.

tools.name: bots.describeBot
tools.description: Provides metadata information about a bot.

tools.name: bots.describeBotAlias
tools.description: Get information about a specific bot alias.

tools.name: bots.describeBotLocale
tools.description: Describes the settings that a bot has for a specific locale.

tools.name: bots.describeBotRecommendation
tools.description: Provides metadata information about a bot recommendation. This information will enable you to get a description on the request inputs, to download associated transcripts after processing is complete, and to download intents and slot-types generated by the bot recommendation.

tools.name: bots.describeBotVersion
tools.description: Provides metadata about a version of a bot.

tools.name: bots.describeCustomVocabularyMetadata
tools.description: Provides metadata information about a custom vocabulary.

tools.name: bots.describeIntent
tools.description: Returns metadata about an intent.

tools.name: bots.describeSlot
tools.description: Gets metadata information about a slot.

tools.name: bots.describeSlotType
tools.description: Gets metadata information about a slot type.

tools.name: bots.listAggregatedUtterances
tools.description:
Provides a list of utterances that users have sent to the bot.

Utterances are aggregated by the text of the utterance. For example, all instances where customers used the phrase "I want to order pizza" are aggregated into the same line in the response.

You can see both detected utterances and missed utterances. A detected utterance is where the bot properly recognized the utterance and activated the associated intent. A missed utterance was not recognized by the bot and didn't activate an intent.

Utterances can be aggregated for a bot alias or for a bot version, but not both at the same time.

Utterances statistics are not generated under the following conditions:

  • The childDirected field was set to true when the bot was created.

  • You are using slot obfuscation with one or more slots.

  • You opted out of participating in improving Amazon Lex.

tools.name: bots.listBotAliases
tools.description: Gets a list of aliases for the specified bot.

tools.name: bots.listBotLocales
tools.description: Gets a list of locales for the specified bot.

tools.name: bots.listBotRecommendations
tools.description: Get a list of bot recommendations that meet the specified criteria.

tools.name: bots.listBots
tools.description: Gets a list of available bots.

tools.name: bots.listBotVersions
tools.description:
Gets information about all of the versions of a bot.

The ListBotVersions operation returns a summary of each version of a bot. For example, if a bot has three numbered versions, the ListBotVersions operation returns for summaries, one for each numbered version and one for the DRAFT version.

The ListBotVersions operation always returns at least one version, the DRAFT version.

tools.name: bots.listCustomVocabularyItems
tools.description: Paginated list of custom vocabulary items for a given bot locale's custom vocabulary.

tools.name: bots.listIntents
tools.description: Get a list of intents that meet the specified criteria.

tools.name: bots.listRecommendedIntents
tools.description: Gets a list of recommended intents provided by the bot recommendation that you can use in your bot. Intents in the response are ordered by relevance.

tools.name: bots.listSlots
tools.description: Gets a list of slots that match the specified criteria.

tools.name: bots.listSlotTypes
tools.description: Gets a list of slot types that match the specified criteria.

tools.name: bots.searchAssociatedTranscripts
tools.description: Search for associated transcripts that meet the specified criteria.

tools.name: bots.startBotRecommendation
tools.description: Use this to provide your transcript data, and to start the bot recommendation process.

tools.name: bots.stopBotRecommendation
tools.description: Stop an already running Bot Recommendation request.

tools.name: bots.updateBot
tools.description: Updates the configuration of an existing bot.

tools.name: bots.updateBotAlias
tools.description: Updates the configuration of an existing bot alias.

tools.name: bots.updateBotLocale
tools.description: Updates the settings that a bot has for a specific locale.

tools.name: bots.updateBotRecommendation
tools.description: Updates an existing bot recommendation request.

tools.name: bots.updateIntent
tools.description: Updates the settings for an intent.

tools.name: bots.updateSlot
tools.description: Updates the settings for a slot.

tools.name: bots.updateSlotType
tools.description: Updates the configuration of an existing slot type.

tools.name: builtins.listBuiltInIntents
tools.description:
Gets a list of built-in intents provided by Amazon Lex that you can use in your bot.

To use a built-in intent as a the base for your own intent, include the built-in intent signature in the parentIntentSignature parameter when you call the CreateIntent operation. For more information, see CreateIntent.

tools.name: builtins.listBuiltInSlotTypes
tools.description: Gets a list of built-in slot types that meet the specified criteria.

tools.name: createuploadurl.createUploadUrl
tools.description: Gets a pre-signed S3 write URL that you use to upload the zip archive when importing a bot or a bot locale.

tools.name: exports.createExport
tools.description:
Creates a zip archive containing the contents of a bot or a bot locale. The archive contains a directory structure that contains JSON files that define the bot.

You can create an archive that contains the complete definition of a bot, or you can specify that the archive contain only the definition of a single bot locale.

For more information about exporting bots, and about the structure of the export archive, see Importing and exporting bots

tools.name: exports.deleteExport
tools.description: Removes a previous export and the associated files stored in an S3 bucket.

tools.name: exports.describeExport
tools.description: Gets information about a specific export.

tools.name: exports.listExports
tools.description: Lists the exports for a bot, bot locale, or custom vocabulary. Exports are kept in the list for 7 days.

tools.name: exports.updateExport
tools.description:
Updates the password used to protect an export zip archive.

The password is not required. If you don't supply a password, Amazon Lex generates a zip file that is not protected by a password. This is the archive that is available at the pre-signed S3 URL provided by the DescribeExport operation.

tools.name: imports.deleteImport
tools.description: Removes a previous import and the associated file stored in an S3 bucket.

tools.name: imports.describeImport
tools.description: Gets information about a specific import.

tools.name: imports.listImports
tools.description: Lists the imports for a bot, bot locale, or custom vocabulary. Imports are kept in the list for 7 days.

tools.name: imports.startImport
tools.description: Starts importing a bot, bot locale, or custom vocabulary from a zip archive that you uploaded to an S3 bucket.

tools.name: policy.createResourcePolicy
tools.description: Creates a new resource policy with the specified policy statements.

tools.name: policy.createResourcePolicyStatement
tools.description:
Adds a new resource policy statement to a bot or bot alias. If a resource policy exists, the statement is added to the current resource policy. If a policy doesn't exist, a new policy is created.

You can't create a resource policy statement that allows cross-account access.

tools.name: policy.deleteResourcePolicy
tools.description: Removes an existing policy from a bot or bot alias. If the resource doesn't have a policy attached, Amazon Lex returns an exception.

tools.name: policy.deleteResourcePolicyStatement
tools.description: Deletes a policy statement from a resource policy. If you delete the last statement from a policy, the policy is deleted. If you specify a statement ID that doesn't exist in the policy, or if the bot or bot alias doesn't have a policy attached, Amazon Lex returns an exception.

tools.name: policy.describeResourcePolicy
tools.description: Gets the resource policy and policy revision for a bot or bot alias.

tools.name: policy.updateResourcePolicy
tools.description: Replaces the existing resource policy for a bot or bot alias with a new one. If the policy doesn't exist, Amazon Lex returns an exception.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Gets a list of tags associated with a resource. Only bots, bot aliases, and bot channels can have tags associated with them.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Adds the specified tags to the specified resource. If a tag key already exists, the existing value is replaced with the new value.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description: Removes tags from a bot, bot alias, or bot channel.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
